task: A genuinely new platform capability: a Card is a shareable, time-boxed unlock -- a Theme Author mints one on World Builder's Welcome screen against a Theme already Published to their Personal Repository, and whoever redeems it (a Pattern Match tap puzzle on Creator's Screen 2, beside World Library) gets that World in the World Library for the exact tries/duration set, never a permanent copy. Redemption uses a randomized rotation/mirror/translation of a real constellation on a 10x10 grid -- the constellation NAME is a reusable flavor label, never the security boundary; the Card's own randomly-placed pattern is what's actually unique. Rarity is always derived from tries x duration, never chosen directly. Only the Builder card type ships; five others stay reserved schema vocabulary. All card logic lives in one new shared js/cardPlatform.js -- Builder and Creator are thin UI hooks. The platform's first RPC/SECURITY DEFINER function, redeem_card(), is the only mechanism that can compare a tap/typed code against the cards table's secret columns without ever exposing them via SELECT; its response never includes pattern/code/serial_no/constellation on any branch, verified mechanically against the real SQL. Three real gaps a wireframe couldn't have caught were resolved: a cross-owner Theme read grant, a new loadPersonalByOwner() lookup, and lazy read-time pruning for time-boxed theme registration. supabase/schema.sql's additions are a disclosed, unexecuted draft. Verified via a new 47-assertion Playwright suite; both goldenBuild.js suites (v1 and v2) pass unchanged. See docs/VIHU_CARD_PLATFORM.md and CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
